name: Chinese Human Connectome Project (CHCP)
description: An HCP-style multimodal neuroimaging resource of 366 Han Chinese adults acquired with protocols nearly identical to the Human Connectome Project. It includes high-resolution structural (T1w/T2w), resting-state and task fMRI (covering the HCP social-cognition/theory-of-mind, emotion, and other paradigms), high-angular diffusion MRI, and behavioral data, enabling cross-cultural comparison of brain organization and social cognition. Led by Jia-Hong Gao at Peking University.
citation: Ge J, Yang G, Han M, et al. (2023). Increasing diversity in connectomics with the Chinese Human Connectome Project. Nature Neuroscience, 26, 163–172.
